response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, embedded, and performance engineering credentials, but his background is almost entirely in application-layer software development. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his resume. His AI-related work at re:cinq is software-level (MCP tooling, agentic workflows) rather than hardware or deep-learning workload optimization, and his educational background (two Bachelor's degrees with no Master's or PhD) does not align with the stated qualification paths for a principal-level hardware-focused role.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering mindset through low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency)
Breadth of systems experience spanning embedded systems, robotics, and hardware-adjacent tooling at Altom Consulting
Exposure to AI/agentic infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of the AI software ecosystem
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or hardware performance simulation — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in GPU architecture, deep neural network training/inference optimization, or AI accelerator workload analysis
Educational profile (two Bachelor's degrees, no graduate degree) does not align with the role's qualification ladder, and no compensating hardware-domain specialization is evident

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload expertise
